name: Structured Brainstorming Matrix
steps: Select 3-5 brainstorming methods from brainstorming-methods.csv; run divergent exploration; cluster angles; keep 2-3 research directions
quality_gate: At least 3 methods used; every direction traces to method output

name: BDD Research Specification
best_for: turning broad direction into testable research questions
steps: Convert directions into GIVEN/WHEN/THEN questions; define in-scope/out-of-scope; define success and failure conditions
quality_gate: Every RQ traceable to pitch; every RQ testable and scoped

name: Engineering Lens Canvas
best_for: technical research, implementation-heavy topics, engineering decisions
steps: Cover first principles, state of art, field methods, architecture, data, metrics, failure modes, cost/latency/complexity, alternatives, open unknowns
quality_gate: Technical report must let an engineer plan implementation without asking basic follow-up

name: Source Taxonomy Planning
steps: Map every RQ to source types from source-taxonomy.csv; assign query families; identify required source diversity
quality_gate: No RQ left unassigned; at least 2 source types for important RQs

name: Multi-Lens Parallel Research
origin: systematic literature review + OSINT + market intelligence
steps: Run internet, social, academic, news, and market lenses in parallel; cite every finding; produce findings per lens
quality_gate: Every finding cited; no silent empty result; queries attempted recorded

name: Implementation Evidence Harvest
origin: engineering due diligence + field practice analysis
steps: Collect GitHub/repos, docs, benchmarks, datasets, model cards, standards, patents, commercial products, deployment case studies
quality_gate: Must include implementable artifacts or explicitly state none found

name: Cross-Source Synthesis
best_for: combining many findings into one narrative
steps: Build claim registry; deduplicate sources; map claims to RQs; resolve contradictions; assign confidence
quality_gate: No claim without citation; contradictions explicit

name: Expert Technical Report
origin: engineering design review + technical feasibility report
best_for: deep technical reports meant for implementation
steps: Write executive summary, first principles, state of art, methods in field, comparison matrix, architecture, roadmap, data strategy, evaluation protocol, failure modes, alternatives, gaps, recommendation
quality_gate: Reader can understand what to build, why, how, risks, and alternatives

name: Falsification Validation
steps: Extract key claims; seek counter-evidence; verify original sources; grade Confirmed/Refuted/Inconclusive
quality_gate: At least one refutation-seeking method per key claim

name: Quality Reflection Rubric
best_for: deciding if report is good enough
steps: Score confidence, coverage, source diversity, technical depth, alternatives, failure modes, implementation actionability
quality_gate: Fail triggers re-research unless user overrides

name: Research Knowledge Closure
steps: Verify artifacts, verdicts, advisor gates, memory update, and closeout summary
quality_gate: No unresolved critical refutation; final artifacts exist
